operations.operation: treat repo as source of truth, chat as ephemeral
operations.decision_rule: files + git history persist; chat clears with /clear or /reset

operations.operation: use tree-sitter repo-map instead of embeddings RAG
operations.decision_rule: repo-map gives LLM symbol-level signatures + PageRank ranking; budget is dynamic (grows when no files /add-ed, shrinks when added)

operations.operation: pick edit format by model capability, not preference
operations.decision_rule: Aider has per-model defaults: GPT-4o/Sonnet=diff, Gemini=diff-fenced, GPT-4-Turbo=udiff, GPT-4.1=patch, weak models=whole

operations.operation: never wrap code edits in JSON tool-calls
operations.trigger: designing agent harness around LLM code edits
operations.decision_rule: use plain-text diff format; JSON degrades every tested model including Sonnet

operations.operation: bootstrap from clean git working tree
operations.decision_rule: cd to repo root, commit/stash in-flight work; aider will auto-commit dirty files separately if you don't
keep added files under 25k tokens; trust repo-map for everything else; /read for reference-only files
use /ask to align on plan; switch to /code (or omit prefix) to execute

operations.operation: use architect mode when reasoner differs from editor
operations.decision_rule: pair architect (o1/o3) with capable editor (Sonnet, GPT-4o, DeepSeek); accept ~2x cost for ~3-6pp benchmark gain
non-zero exit feeds output back to LLM which proposes fix; wrap reformatters in dual-pass script

operations.trigger: team or project has consistent style preferences
operations.decision_rule: load once via --read; example: 'prefer httpx over requests; use type hints'
cd into subdir, run aider with --subtree-only; exclude vendor/dist with .aiderignore

operations.operation: /clear when topic shifts; /reset for clean slate
operations.trigger: long session drift or new task
operations.decision_rule: /clear keeps files, drops chat; /reset drops both

operations.trigger: LLM proposes edits to unintended/invented file path
operations.decision_rule: /ls to inspect; /add target; /drop extras; /ask which file implements X if unknown

operations.dilemma: context window overflow on large repo
diff format hallucinates / SEARCH block not found
operations.trigger: Aider reports 'SEARCH block not found' repeatedly
operations.decision_rule: /clear; upgrade model; fallback --edit-format whole; try --architect for two-step reliability

operations.dilemma: is architect mode worth the cost
operations.trigger: considering whether to pay for two LLM calls per turn
operations.decision_rule: skip if reasoner edits cleanly; use if reasoner is lazy at editing (o1-preview 79.7%->82.7% with Sonnet); SOTA path (85%) is too slow for interactive

operations.trigger: Claude 3.5 Sonnet response cuts off mid-edit
operations.decision_rule: Aider already handles via multi-response stitching + minimality prompts; update Aider; if persists, manually request minimal SEARCH/REPLACE blocks

operations.dilemma: JSON tool-call vs plain text diff for code
operations.trigger: designing agent harness; weighing structured output
operations.decision_rule: prefer plain-text diff; JSON degrades every tested model; if forced, single string field, expect quality hit

operations.anti_pattern: use Aider for greenfield with no existing codebase
operations.rationale: repo-map empty; per-edit-commit safety has no marginal value over plain LLM chat

operations.anti_pattern: use Aider for refactors across thousands of files
operations.rationale: 25k-token sweet spot for added files; sed/AST/IDE refactor first then Aider for semantic residual
loses per-edit commit, /undo, dirty-file separation, audit trail

operations.anti_pattern: use Aider for fully autonomous ticket->PR
operations.rationale: Aider is human-in-loop by design; OpenHands/Devin target autonomy

operations.fix: /drop aggressively; trust repo-map (70.3% correct-file hit on SWE-Bench Lite)

operations.mistake: weak model as editor in architect mode
operations.fix: editor must follow edit format reliably; Sonnet/DeepSeek/GPT-4o are good editors
/clear when topic shifts; long history drifts model behavior

operations.mistake: disable auto-commit to keep history clean
operations.fix: leave auto-commit on; squash with git rebase -i HEAD~N at end
load with --read; persists style decisions across sessions

operations.decision: Aider = retrospective /undo; Cline = prospective per-tool-call approve
Aider = terminal pure + open-source; Cursor = closed IDE + visual diff overlays
Continue = cross-IDE (VS Code + JetBrains); Aider = terminal-only
Aider = human-in-loop REPL; OpenHands = autonomous sandbox ticket->PR

operations.research_legacy: Aider's published experiments inform any coding-agent designer
items: udiff vs SEARCH/REPLACE: 20% -> 61% on GPT-4 Turbo refactor benchmark
tree-sitter repo-map: 70.3% correct-file hit on SWE-Bench Lite
JSON tool-call wrapping degrades every tested model for code editing
